rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope only when binding implementation-level details; use component type scope for abstract input/output contracts.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by anchoring AGREE contracts to visible features, not subcomponent paths or internal implementation details.
Only reference data ports, constants, or properties explicitly defined in the AADL model; avoid inferred or derived identifiers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for all AGREE contracts, with implementation annexes limited to bindings after subcomponents and connections.
Boolean range expressions must use real or integer literals matching the port's declared type; never coerce Boolean or opaque ports into numeric comparisons.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables; they are not in scope without explicit typed ports or constants.
Avoid using subcomponent port names in component type annexes; only use top-level component features.
